title: Synthesis Layer — Entity Extraction, Cross-Reference, and Timeline
description: Can a useful entity graph be derived from existing photo and document manifests? Three-way branch comparison on person name deduplication (string normalization vs fuzzy/phonetic vs LLM clustering) — the core empirical question given inconsistent romanized Chinese names across documents.
key_findings: Data recon: people registry is entirely unpopulated (794 unnamed face clusters)
Data recon: same person appears as 3-4 name variants across documents (romanization inconsistency)
Data recon: photo people_notes is prose description, not names — person-photo linking requires face clusters, not manifests

person_dedup.a: String normalization (lowercase, strip hyphens, collapse whitespace)
person_dedup.c: LLM-assisted clustering (one Claude call to group name variants)
